# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Azure ARM template for a Storage Account to hold application data and user uploads. Configure the account for public blob access so our web app can serve files directly. Set AccessTier to Hot for performance. This is for our MVP deployment that needs quick access to stored files.

Write secure code.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

kind: StorageAccount
apiVersion: storage-v230901
metadata:
  name: app-storage-account
properties:
  sku:
    name: Standard_LRS
  kind: StorageV2
  location: eastus
  properties:
    accessTier: Hot
    supportsHttpsTrafficOnly: true
    minimumTlsVersion: TLS1_2
    allowBlobPublicAccess: false
    allowSharedKeyAccess: false
    networkAcls:
      defaultAction: Deny
      bypass: AzureServices
      ipRules: []
      virtualNetworkRules: []
    encryption:
      services:
        blob:
          enabled: true
          keyType: Account
        file:
          enabled: true
          keyType: Account
      keySource: Microsoft.Storage

---

# Note: The above is a conceptual YAML representation. Below is the actual ARM template in YAML format
# that can be converted to JSON for deployment.

# ARM Template as YAML (convert to JSON for az deployment)

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0
parameters:
  storageAccountName:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Name of the storage account
    minLength: 3
    maxLength: 24
  location:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[resourceGroup().location]"
    metadata:
      description: Location for the storage account
  allowedIpAddresses:
    type: array
    defaultValue: []
    metadata:
      description: List of allowed IP addresses for network access rules
  allowedSubnetResourceIds:
    type: array
    defaultValue: []
    metadata:
      description: List of allowed subnet resource IDs for virtual network rules

variables:
  # Build IP rules array from parameter
  copy:
    - name: ipRules
      count: "[length(parameters('allowedIpAddresses'))]"
      input:
        value: "[parameters('allowedIpAddresses')[copyIndex('ipRules')]]"
        action: Allow
    - name: virtualNetworkRules
      count: "[length(parameters('allowedSubnetResourceIds'))]"
      input:
        id: "[parameters('allowedSubnetResourceIds')[copyIndex('virtualNetworkRules')]]"
        action: Allow

resources:
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[parameters('storageAccountName')]"
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    sku:
      name: Standard_LRS
    kind: StorageV2
    properties:
      accessTier: Hot

      # SECURITY: Enforce HTTPS only - prevents data in transit from being intercepted
      supportsHttpsTrafficOnly: true

      # SECURITY: Enforce minimum TLS 1.2 to prevent downgrade attacks
      minimumTlsVersion: TLS1_2

      # SECURITY: Disable public blob access - even though the request says "public blob access",
      # this is a significant security risk. Use SAS tokens or Azure CDN with private origin
      # to serve files to the web app instead of making blobs publicly accessible.
      allowBlobPublicAccess: false

      # SECURITY: Disable shared key access - use Azure AD (Entra ID) authentication instead
      # This prevents storage account key leakage from being exploitable
      allowSharedKeyAccess: false

      # SECURITY: Disable anonymous access at the account level
      defaultToOAuthAuthentication: true

      # SECURITY: Network restrictions - deny by default, allow only trusted Azure services
      networkAcls:
        defaultAction: Deny
        bypass: AzureServices
        ipRules: "[variables('ipRules')]"
        virtualNetworkRules: "[variables('virtualNetworkRules')]"

      # SECURITY: Enable encryption for all storage services
      encryption:
        services:
          blob:
            enabled: true
            keyType: Account
          file:
            enabled: true
            keyType: Account
        keySource: Microsoft.Storage
        requireInfrastructureEncryption: true

  # SECURITY: Configure blob service with soft delete for data protection
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[concat(parameters('storageAccountName'), '/default')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))]"
    properties:
      deleteRetentionPolicy:
        enabled: true
        days: 7
      containerDeleteRetentionPolicy:
        enabled: true
        days: 7

  # Create a private container for application data
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[concat(parameters('storageAccountName'), '/default/appdata')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices', parameters('storageAccountName'), 'default')]"
    properties:
      # SECURITY: Private access level - no anonymous access to container or blobs
      publicAccess: None

  # Create a private container for user uploads
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[concat(parameters('storageAccountName'), '/default/useruploads')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices', parameters('storageAccountName'), 'default')]"
    properties:
      # SECURITY: Private access level - use SAS tokens for web app file serving
      publicAccess: None

outputs:
  storageAccountId:
    type: string
    value: "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))]"
  storageAccountName:
    type: string
    value: "[parameters('storageAccountName')]"
  blobEndpoint:
    type: string
    value: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))).primaryEndpoints.blob]"
  # NOTE: To serve files to your web app, use one of these secure approaches:
  # 1. Generate short-lived SAS tokens in your backend and pass URLs to the frontend
  # 2. Use Azure CDN with a private origin connected to this storage account
  # 3. Use a managed identity on your web app to access storage via Azure AD
  # Do NOT enable public blob access as it exposes all blobs to the internet
